body_txt: Basically, this is any child character explaining to their teacher why they haven't done his homework. This will be either a lie, which may or may not be believed, or a Cassandra Truth . If it is the latter, expect them to also bring in the evidence proving their case (such as moist bits from homework, or even the animal itself). Hilarity can ensue if a student comes to school with their hand in said dog’s mouth from an attempt to retrieve their homework. The most common variant involves a dog, but other animals can be used as well. Though this has become a Dead Horse Trope , and children rarely use this excuse seriously, the second variation on this trope is in fact Truth in Television . Many dogs do have a thing for chewing or eating paper, or are just Extreme Omnivores . Puppies in either of their teething phases (getting baby teeth at 3-4 weeks, or getting adult teeth several months later) are also notorious for chewing up anything they can get their mouths on.
